2605.08685.title: Event Fields: Learning Latent Event Structure for Waveform Foundation Models
2605.08685.paper_summary: We propose a new class of waveform foundation models that departs from conventional sequence based representations by modeling physiological time series as realizations of latent event processes. Rather than treating signals as collections of local tokens or patches, our approach assumes that clinically meaningful structure arises from temporally extended, interacting events whose boundaries and dynamics are not directly observed. To capture this structure, we introduce a self supervised learning framework that enforces consistency across stochastic segmentations and time frequency projections of the same waveform, encouraging representations that are invariant to signal level perturbations while preserving event level organization. The resulting model combines a segmentation aware encoder with a latent interaction operator that captures dependencies among inferred events, and naturally extends to multimodal settings by aligning modalities through shared event representations. Across a range of physiological benchmarks, including arrhythmia classification, hemodynamic prediction, and waveform retrieval, the proposed method improves performance, robustness, and label efficiency relative to strong sequence based baselines. These results suggest that shifting from signal centric to event centric representations provides a more appropriate inductive bias for modeling physiological dynamics and offers a complementary path to scaling foundation models in healthcare.
2605.08685.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種新的波形基礎模型類別，這種模型不同於傳統的基於序列的表示，通過將生理時間序列建模為潛在事件過程的實現來實現。<lb/>我們的方法假設臨床上有意義的結構源於時間上延伸的、相互作用的事件，這些事件的邊界和動態並不是直接觀察到的，而不是將信號視為局部標記或補丁的集合。<lb/>為了捕捉這種結構，我們引入了一個自我監督學習框架，該框架強制在相同波形的隨機分段和時間頻率投影之間保持一致性，鼓勵對信號級擾動不變的表示，同時保留事件級的組織。<lb/>最終模型結合了一個關注分段的編碼器和一個潛在交互運算符，該運算符捕捉推斷事件之間的依賴關係，並通過共享事件表示自然擴展到多模態設置。<lb/>在一系列生理基準測試中，包括心律失常分類、血流動力學預測和波形檢索，所提出的方法相對於強大的基於序列的基準提高了性能、穩健性和標籤效率。<lb/>這些結果表明，從以信號為中心轉向以事件為中心的表示為建模生理動力學提供了更合適的歸納偏見，並為在醫療保健中擴展基礎模型提供了一條互補的途徑。
2605.08685.author: Li Na et.al.
2605.08685.authors: Li Na, Yuanyun Zhang, Shi Li
